authorityBoundary: Rows distinguish source authority and variation; they do not assert legal equivalence, clinical adequacy, or organisational compliance.

layers.label: National consistency layer
layers.description: Candidate shared process concerns across jurisdictions: identify actual or potential harm, communicate with affected people, support participation, review learning, and improve systems.

layers.label: New Zealand consumer-rights requirements
layers.description: Consumer communication, information, support, and complaint rights arise from the Code; the adverse-events policy adds a national reporting, review, learning, and improvement policy layer.

layers.label: Australian national framework and accreditation layer
layers.description: The national framework provides a nationally consistent basis for open disclosure, while NSQHS Action 1.12 describes an accreditation-standard expectation for an open disclosure program and monitoring.

layers.label: NSW incident-management overlay
layers.description: NSW-specific incident reporting, risk assessment, review, and learning procedures must be treated as a state policy overlay rather than as the Australian national framework.

layers.label: NSW open-disclosure overlay
layers.description: NSW open disclosure is modeled as a state policy overlay linked to incident management; the exact current directive text and applicability must be verified before obligation encoding.

layers.label: Regional implementation
layers.description: District, facility, or service-level procedures may implement a national or state source but are not generalized here as national or jurisdiction-wide requirements.

layers.label: Hypothetical local procedure
layers.description: A local workflow, role assignment, escalation route, or documentation practice without a named authoritative source remains an explicit modeling boundary, not an inferred obligation.

actorAuthority.discretion: may express needs, preferences, questions, and whether to participate in a discussion
actorAuthority.escalationOwner: service-specific human owner to be identified from controlling local policy

actorAuthority.discretion: implements the applicable policy or standard within jurisdictional and local constraints
actorAuthority.escalationOwner: clinical-governance or equivalent accountable role; exact role is jurisdiction-specific

actorAuthority.discretion: selects or applies a review pathway only where the controlling jurisdictional source supports it
actorAuthority.escalationOwner: not inferred; requires jurisdictional source and local procedure

promotionBoundary: All rows remain agent-proposed; no row is a certified obligation or legal/clinical recommendation.
